economic_notes: BCN guarantees loans for subsidiaries and partnerships totalling $23,806,710. Related entities include Bigstone Business Trust, Bigstone Health Commission Business Trust, Bigstone Cree Nation Business Trust, Bigstone Economic Holdings, Bigstone Health Holdings, and Bigstone Forestry Inc. Operations in Wabasca-Desmarais and neighboring territories in northern Alberta.
notes: Audit was qualified due to absence of budget information as required by Canadian public sector accounting standards. Financial statements are non-consolidated; six related entities (Bigstone Business Trust, Bigstone Health Commission Business Trust, Bigstone Cree Nation Business Trust, Bigstone Economic Holdings, Bigstone Health Holdings, Bigstone Forestry Inc.) are accounted for at cost rather than consolidated. Fifteen additional related entities are not included at all. Deferred revenue increased dramatically from $16.4M to $41.1M, largely driven by ISC ($29.2M) and CMHC ($7.3M) deferred transfers. BCN was not in compliance with a reporting covenant on its CIBC loans subsequent to year-end. The accumulated surplus figure shown in note 12 ($74,169,248) differs slightly from the statement ($74,340,402) — likely a rounding/typo in the notes. OSR calculated as total revenue $62,714,879 minus net government transfers recognized $50,000,321 = $12,714,558; however using gross government transfers note 13 total $74,220,927 subtracted from total revenue $62,714,879 yields negative — OSR calculated as total revenue minus gross government transfers recognized in revenue (net amounts after deferrals and repayable): $62,714,879 - $50,000,321 = $12,714,558.
